channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Tehran and Trump Send Mixed Signals Ahead of Potential Talks
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is set to lead a U.S. delegation to Pakistan as Mr. Trump pushes for a peace deal. Iranian officials said privately they would send a team if Mr. Vance were there.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: A Leaner Saudi Arabia Turns From Grandiose Plans to Pragmatism
item.description: A decade after Mohammed bin Salman unveiled his “Vision 2030” program to transform the country’s economy, the kingdom is facing financial strains and reassessing its trajectory.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Saudi crown prince, Mohammed bin Salman, in Washington last fall. The prince’s plans and ambitions have grown more rapidly than the state’s financial capacity.

item.title: Javier Milei Tamed Argentina’s Inflation. Now He Wants to Reshape Its Values.
item.description: Argentina’s right-wing president has tamed the country’s runaway inflation. Now he wants to transform its values.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola and Sarah Pabst
item.media:description: Javier Milei in Mar del Plata, Argentina, in January at the Derecha Fest, a large right-wing gathering.

item.title: Ukraine, Short on Troops, Is Turning to Robots to Help Its War Efforts
item.description: Ukraine is using unmanned ground vehicles armed with bombs, guns or rockets to carry out attacks and keep its soldiers out of harm’s way.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova
item.media:description: Ukrainian soldiers training with an unmanned ground vehicle in the Donbas region of eastern Ukraine last year. The country is eager to highlight its advances in technological warfare.

item.title: Starmer Voices Fury Over Being Kept in the Dark on Mandelson Vetting
item.description: During intense grilling in Parliament, Prime Minister Keir Starmer blamed civil servants for not telling him that Peter Mandelson had been rejected for top security clearances.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: A photograph released by the UK Parliament showing Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain at the House of Commons on Monday.

item.title: Pakistan’s Leaders Try to Contain Rising Anger Over Iran War at Home
item.description: With deep spiritual ties to Iran, Pakistan’s minority Shiites are angry about the killing of Iran’s top clerics in U.S.-Israeli strikes, complicating Pakistan’s role as mediator.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman and Asim Hafeez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Asim Hafeez for The New York Times

item.title: Tsunami Warnings Downgraded in Japan After Strong Offshore Earthquake
item.description: The 7.7-magnitude undersea quake occurred off Iwate Prefecture, on the northeastern coast of Japan’s main island, Honshu.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.media:description: A tsunami warning is displayed on a television in Japan after a strong earthquake off the country’s northern coast on Monday.

item.title: A Decisive Win Raises Bulgarians’ Hopes for Change
item.description: An election victory by a former president, Rumen Radev, gives the country a chance for stability and to clean out the corruption that has stymied its growth.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall
item.media:description: Rumen Radev, Bulgaria’s former president, at a polling site in Sofia, Bulgaria, on Sunday. His coalition, Progressive Bulgaria, secured a majority of seats in Parliament in Sunday’s election.

item.title: Israeli Soldier in Lebanon Sledgehammered a Statue of Jesus
item.description: The military is investigating the soldier. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu expressed regret for any hurt caused to “believers in Lebanon and around the world.”
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger

item.title: Mexico to Investigate Security Role of 2 U.S. Officials Killed in Crash
item.description: President Claudia Sheinbaum said the inquiry would focus on whether the involvement of the Americans in a drug-fighting operation violated Mexico’s national security laws and sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas
item.media:description: A suspected methamphetamine processing lab discovered in northern Mexico’s Chihuahua state.

item.title: Elon Musk Ignores French Prosecutors, Widening Tech Rift With Europe
item.description: Prosecutors investigating his social media company, X, had summoned him for a meeting. His no-show reflected a broader dispute over regulation.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter
item.media:description: Elon Musk speaking in Switzerland in January. He has previously denounced the French investigation.

item.title: The Iran War Sent Shock Waves Through Asia That Are Likely to Spread
item.description: The Asia-Pacific was hit hard and quick by the war in Iran and its energy bottlenecks. Scenes of crisis there indicate that problems are multiplying and spreading.
item.dc:creator: Damien Cave
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times
item.media:description: A floating fuel station selling diesel last month in the Mekong River delta. Vietnam has been hit hard by rising fuel costs because of the war in Iran.

item.title: In Turkey, Middle Powers Ponder Diplomacy With a Rogue U.S.
item.description: The U.S. remains an essential player. The problem, one analyst said, is how to deal effectively with a power that is “indispensable, coercive and unpredictable at the same time.”
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan of Turkey in Antalya on Friday, where he said the global system was in “a moral and existential crisis.”

item.title: Gen Z Looks to Nepal as a Test Case of Its Hopes
item.description: Many youth-led protests around the world have failed to bring meaningful change. But in Nepal, a new government is promising to do things differently.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech, Binod Ghimire, Sajal Pradhan and Hannah Reyes Morales
item.media:description: A youthful revolution changed Nepal. It gave hope to Gen Z worldwide.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Tyler Pager, Farnaz Fassihi and Erika Solomon

item.title: London Braces for Disruption From Tube Drivers’ Strike
item.description: The dispute over working conditions and hours is expected to cause widespread delays, stopping service on two lines.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: A sign warning travelers of strike-related delays at the Stratford Underground station in London on Monday.

item.title: Japan to Sell More Weapons Abroad, Breaking With Postwar Pacifism
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi reversed limits on arms exports as Japan faces rising threats from China and unpredictability from its main ally, the United States.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: A Japanese marine unit takes part in exercises in Tokunoshima, Japan, in 2023.

item.title: Iran’s Nonnuclear Deterrent
item.description: Disrupting the Strait of Hormuz turned out to be a powerful weapon. It might be a new way for Iran to keep adversaries at bay, even without a nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Facing Hard-Liners at Home and Bluster From Trump, Iran Sends Mixed Signals on Peace Talks
item.description: Publicly, Iranian officials refuse to confirm a second round of negotiations with the United States, even as they privately say they are making plans to attend.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman walking by a mural showing Iranian missiles attacking a U.S. ship, in Tehran in April.

item.title: Canadian Tourist Is Killed in Shooting at Mexico’s Pyramids
item.description: A gunman opened fire at the Teotihuacán archaeological site outside Mexico City before killing himself, the authorities said. Six Americans were among those injured, including two from gunshots.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Cyntia Barrera Díaz
item.media:description: Police officers at the Pyramid of the Moon at the Teotihuacán pyramids in Mexico after a deadly shooting on Monday.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Talks Appear on Track to Resume Despite Mixed Messages
item.description: With the two-week cease-fire almost over, Vice President JD Vance was expected to head to Pakistan on Tuesday for the second round of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Farnaz Fassihi, Tyler Pager and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance in Islamabad, Pakistan, this month during a previous round of negotiations between the United States and Iran.

item.title: The Touska, the Iranian-Flagged Ship Seized by U.S. Forces, Was Under Sanctions
item.description: The container ship, the Touska, was sanctioned by the U.S. Treasury over links to Iranian weapons programs.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A screenshot of a video posted by U.S. Central Command on Sunday that was described as showing a U.S. Navy destroyer near an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Arabian Sea.

item.title: The Touska, the Iranian-Flagged Ship Seized by U.S. Forces, Was Under Sanctions
item.description: Marines are searching thousands of containers aboard the Touska, an Iranian cargo ship that the Navy disabled and seized on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, last week.

item.title: In Lebanon, residents of the south make their way home over signs of war.
item.description: Some of the million people displaced by the latest war between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon are flooding back home since a 10-day truce between Israel and Lebanon took hold.
item.dc:creator: David Guttenfelder
item.media:description: A Lebanese boy is welcomed by his aunt and uncle after crossing a destroyed bridge with other family members, in the background. They were crossing over the Litani River in the south of Lebanon near the village of Tayr Felsay, on Monday.

item.title: How Bruce the Parrot Landed Atop the Pecking Order, Without a Beak
item.description: The kea gained fame for learning to use a pebble to groom himself. Scientists were astounded by his next innovation.
item.dc:creator: Carl Zimmer

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Is at a Standstill Again
item.description: Few vessels are crossing after Iran reversed course on reopening the vital waterway and ships came under attack.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships and boats in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, on Monday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Farnaz Fassihi and Max Bearak

item.title: 2 U.S. Government Workers Among 4 Killed in Car Crash in Mexico
item.description: Two of the victims were American and two were Mexican, the authorities in Chihuahua said. The accident took place on a treacherous mountain road.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas
item.media:credit: Attorney General'S Office of Chihuahua, via, via Reuters
item.media:description: A suspected methamphetamine processing lab discovered in northern Mexico’s Chihuahua state.

item.title: A Humanoid Robot Races to a Record Half-Marathon Finish
item.description: The android won a race featuring robots and humans on Sunday in Beijing, achieving a technological milestone while finishing faster than any person in history.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:description: The 5-foot-5 humanoid robot Lightning, with its approximately 3-foot-long legs striding, showed its form in a half-marathon race on Sunday in Beijing.

item.title: Messy War, Happy Stock Markets
item.description: On the surface, the recent rally doesn’t seem to make sense: Oil prices are high and a peace deal with Iran remains elusive. Here’s what’s going on.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Timothy A. Clary/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Can U.S. Blockade Iranian-Linked Ships Anywhere in the World? Yes, But …
item.description: Maritime and military law experts say an expansion of the naval blockade announced last week raises legal and practical questions but has ample historical precedent.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tankers anchored off Iran on Saturday.

item.title: Energy Secretary Says Gas Prices May Stay Above $3 Until 2027
item.description: Secretary of Energy Chris Wright’s acknowledgment in a TV interview undercut President Trump’s earlier claim that price increases would be “short-term.”
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim and Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The price of gas has increased significantly since the war with Iran began in late February.

item.title: U.S. Attacks Iranian Cargo Ship as Both Countries Claim Cease-Fire Violations
item.description: President Trump said the United States attacked an Iranian ship that tried to get past the U.S. blockade, hours after Iran said the blockade was a war crime that violated the cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump speaking to reporter on Air Force One.

item.title: Hamas Officials Say Group Is Ready to Hand Over Some Weapons
item.description: Two senior Hamas officials in Gaza said the group was prepared to relinquish some automatic rifles and other arms, a concession that falls short of Israeli-U.S. demands.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer and Adam Rasgon
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Armed members of Hamas in Nuseirat, Gaza, last year.

item.title: British Counterterrorism Police Investigating Attacks Against Jewish Sites
item.description: The police say they are focusing on a shadowy Islamic group that may have links to Iran and which has claimed responsibility for several recent arson attacks.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.media:description: Police forensic officers worked Sunday inside a cordon set up near Kenton United Synagogue. The synagogue, in Harrow, northwest London, was the scene of an overnight arson attack.

item.title: Fire Destroys Most of Coastal Village in Malaysia
item.description: A community of stilt homes on the island of Borneo was reduced to ash in an overnight blaze.
item.dc:creator: Zunaira Saieed
item.media:credit: Malaysia’s Sandakan Fire And Rescue Department, via Agence France-Presse
item.media:description: A view of Kampung Bahagia, on the island of Borneo in Malaysia, in a handout photo taken and released by Malaysia’s Sandakan Fire and Rescue Department on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Eases Some Internet Restrictions, as Wider Blackout Passes 50th Day
item.description: Critics say Iran may be creating a “tiered internet” model, where access is limited to the politically and economically privileged.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Two women with their phones in Tehran, Iran, on Thursday.

item.title: In Qatar, Trapped Between the U.S. and Iran, War Forced a Reckoning
item.description: The gas-rich Gulf nation is in a state of “strategic shock” after the war dealt a serious blow to its economy, sending ripples around the world.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Qatar, though on good terms with both the United States and Iran, has been harmed economically by the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran and endured retaliatory attacks from Tehran.

item.title: How Gen Z Protests Have Fared Around the World
item.description: Over the past year or so, young people have rallied across continents to oust old-guard governments. What’s happened since?
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Young people at a concert last year celebrating the impeachment of President Andry Rajoelina of Madagascar.

item.title: Syrian Billionaires Needed a Favor in Washington. They Invoked the Trump Name.
item.description: The attempt by the Khayyats to influence foreign policy while discussions are underway about potential Trump family deals is an increasingly common feature of the president’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Eric Lipton
item.media:credit: Laura Boushnak for The New York Times
item.media:description: Moutaz and Ramez Al-Khayyat, two of three Syrian-born brothers, at their company’s headquarters in Doha, Qatar.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Shirin Hakim and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: Bulgarian Voters Back New Political Coalition Led by Radev
item.description: With its citizens yearning for the kind of prosperous life enjoyed by other Europeans, Bulgaria held its eighth election in five years.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall
item.media:description: Former President Rumen Radev of Bulgaria at a polling station in Sofia on Sunday.

item.title: Hezbollah Is Willing to Cooperate With a Cease-Fire With Israel. For Now.
item.description: The leader of the Iran-backed militia said that a more durable cease-fire with Israel would require the fulfillment of a list of longstanding demands.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Protesters waved an Iranian flag and flags for Hezbollah in downtown Beirut during a demonstration to show support for Hezbollah last week.

item.title: U.S. Officials Visited Havana to Lay Out Proposals for Cuban Reforms
item.description: The delegation told Cuba’s leadership that it had only a narrow window of time to make the economic and political changes demanded by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:description: A street in Havana last month. During the talks last week, a State Department official said, the American delegation insisted that President Trump was open to reaching a diplomatic solution to the crisis.

item.title: Kyiv Mass Shooting Kills 6 and Gunman Is Shot Dead After Taking Hostages in Grocery
item.description: It was the deadliest mass shooting in Ukraine in years, where firearms have proliferated since the war with Russia began.
item.dc:creator: Constant Méheut
item.media:description: Outside the supermarket in Kyiv, Ukraine, where a man took hostages and was killed by the police on Saturday.

item.title: Ships Attacked in Strait of Hormuz as Iran Declares Strict Control of Vital Shipping Route
item.description: The day after Iran declared the vital waterway open, it reversed course, injecting new peril into navigation there.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tankers anchored in the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday.

item.title: Six Are Missing After Cargo Ship Is Found Overturned Near Guam
item.description: The ship, the Mariana, was found overturned days after the Coast Guard lost contact with its crew, after a super typhoon struck.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker
item.media:credit: U.S. Coast Guard, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The U.S. Coast Guard is searching for six crew members from the Mariana, a U.S.-registered dry cargo vessel, which was found overturned in the Pacific.

item.title: More Displaced Lebanese Head Home as Cease-Fire Is Tested
item.description: The truce appeared to mostly hold even as Israel said it had carried out strikes on what it called “terrorists” approaching its forces in southern Lebanon. Separately, a U.N. peacekeeper was killed.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Sarah Chaayto and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Displaced Lebanese residents returned to their homes in the south on Saturday.

item.title: Pope Says News Outlets Misread Some of His Remarks as Criticism of Trump
item.description: Pope Leo responded directly on Monday to a presidential attack. But since then, he said, some of his statements during his Africa trip have been misconstrued.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: El papa León XIV en Yaundé, capital de Camerún, el sábado.

item.title: Trading Spaces
item.description: With spring comes the urge to spring-clean. It’s about so much more than just organizing your closet.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Carney’s Liberal Majority Reshapes Fortunes to the Left and the Right
item.description: Mark Carney’s brand of centrist politics and his invitation to floor crossers with conservative convictions into the Liberal Party’s ranks have reshaped the country’s political economy.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:credit: Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney with new members of the Liberal caucus, from left: Tatiana Auguste (Terrebonne), Danielle Martin (University-Rosedale) and Doly Begum (Scarborough Southwest) on Wednesday in Ottawa.

item.title: For Iran, Flexing Control Over Waterway Is New Deterrent
item.description: Iran’s government could emerge from the conflict with a blueprint to keep adversaries at bay, regardless of any restrictions on its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Adam Entous and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A satellite image showing ships’ movements in a section of the Strait of Hormuz this month.

item.title: Who Is the New Leader of Hungary?
item.description: Our reporter Andrew Higgins describes why Hungary’s voters chose Peter Magyar over Viktor Orban in a landslide, ending Orban’s 16 years in power.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins, Christina Thornell and Leila Medina

item.title: Maduro’s Successor Is Purging Allies Who Kept Him in Power in Venezuela
item.description: The successor to Venezuela’s captured President Nicolás Maduro is purging the people who kept him in power.
item.dc:creator: Anatoly Kurmanaev and Mariana Martínez
item.media:description: Delcy Rodríguez, Venezuela’s former vice president, in January in Caracas, the capital.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Euan Ward, Leily Nikounazar and Somini Sengupta

item.title: In Angola, Pope Leo XIV Faces the Legacy of Colonialism
item.description: His visit includes a trip to a shrine where enslaved Africans were baptized before being forced into the treacherous voyage across the Atlantic Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Cláudio C. Silva and John Eligon
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Luanda, Angola, on Saturday. Angola’s is the oldest Catholic community in southern Africa, dating back more than 500 years.
